section.heading: Comparison of paper and envelopes with standard quality

content.p: The Director of the Government Publishing Office shall compare every lot of paper and envelopes delivered by a contractor with the standard of quality fixed upon by the Joint Committee on Printing, and may not accept paper or envelopes which do not conform to it in every particular. A lot of delivered paper or envelopes which does not conform to the standard of quality may be accepted by the Committee at a discount that in its opinion is sufficient to protect the interests of the Government.

note.heading: Historical and Revision Notes
note.p: Based on 44 U.S. Code, 1964 ed., § 9 (Jan. 12, 1895, ch. 23, § 7, 28 Stat. 602; June 20, 1936, ch. 630, title VIII, § 13, 49 Stat. 1553).

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2014—Pub. L. 113–235 substituted “Director of the Government Publishing Office” for “Public Printer”.
